title: Data Handlers
author: Gallagher, James
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: This presentation was part of a 2-day workshop provided for the Australian Bureau of Meteorology during 15-16 October 2007. The workshop targets system administrators whose task is to setup a data service for interactive users, batch processes, and high level web services, that will access netcdf, hdf, or freeform data in a file system or SQL data in a database. The workshop provides them with the information they need to install and configure Hyrax along with some background on available clients so they can test their servers and give basic advice to users. Summary of the slide deck: The presentation delves into Data Handlers within the context of the BES (Backend Server) for OPeNDAP systems, elucidating their role as specialized modules adept at parsing data formats and generating DAP2 objects. It outlines the process of obtaining these handlers, whether through binary downloads or building from source, with detailed instructions provided for both methods. Additionally, it elucidates the configuration steps necessary for integrating these handlers into the BES, including modifications to the bes.conf file and setting appropriate parameters. The presentation further covers the restart procedure for Hyrax following configuration changes and offers methods to verify successful implementation. Furthermore, it introduces FreeForm, a tool for serving diverse data formats, providing insights into its usage, format file creation, handling of headers, and additional attributes. The presentation culminates with practical examples and resources for further exploration, enhancing comprehension of Data Handlers and FreeForm within the OPeNDAP ecosystem.
